data.description: 大家好，我想就近期 GooseCode（@Minecraftgoose）发布的 GooseHyperGlass 项目与我之前移植的 liquid-glass-webgl 之间的关系，做一个客观、完整的说明，以便社区厘清事实。 背景 我在发现 Kyant 的 AndroidLiquidGlass 项目后，认为其视觉效果与设计理念非常出色，于是投入约两周时间、数十小时将其移植到 Web 端。由于移植过程中几乎全程使用 Z.AI agent 辅助，我将主要精力放在了效果还原与...

    <script type="application/ld+json">{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"DiscussionForumPosting","headline":"关于 GooseHyperGlass 项目与 liquid-glass-webgl 移植仓库的关系说明","articleBody":"大家好，我想就近期 GooseCode（[@Minecraftgoose](https://github.com/Minecraftgoose)）发布的 [GooseHyperGlass](https://github.com/Minecraftgoose/GooseHyperGlass) 项目与我之前移植的 [liquid-glass-webgl](https://github.com/martin65536/liquid-glass-webgl) 之间的关系，做一个客观、完整的说明，以便社区厘清事实。\n\n---\n\n背景\n\n我在发现 Kyant 的 [AndroidLiquidGlass](https://github.com/Kyant0/AndroidLiquidGlass) 项目后，认为其视觉效果与设计理念非常出色，于是投入约两周时间、数十小时将其移植到 Web 端。由于移植过程中几乎全程使用 Z.AI agent 辅助，我将主要精力放在了效果还原与性能优化上，疏忽了仓库的文档建设，初期未添加 `README.md` 和 `LICENSE` 文件。但我在 [Bilibili（BV14ZNn6QED5）](https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV14ZNn6QED5) 和 Kimi 社区等平台的发布页面中，均将原作者 Kyant 的署名置于最显著位置，不存在任何隐藏原作者的意图。（Kimi 社区页面无直接分享链接，相关截图可见下方附件。）\n\n---\n\n事件经过\n\n1. 技术交流的初衷\n\n   我注意到 GooseCode 官网的圆角实现存在明显瑕疵，出于技术交流的目的，我主动向 GooseCode 建议可以参考我移植仓库中的 G2 连续曲率圆角实现。\n\n2. 对方的回应\n\n   对方并未接受建议，反而质疑我移植版本的性能，声称“用了 WebGL 就肯定在 A9 设备上无法流畅运行”。\n\n3. 我的表态\n\n   我在此背景下表示，欢迎对方做出更流畅、更优质的实现。\n\n4. GooseHyperGlass 的发布与疑似洗稿\n\n   随后 GooseCode 发布了 GooseHyperGlass 项目。经对比，该项目在 Shader 代码结构、核心算法等方面存在大量与我基于 Z.AI 移植的版本高度相似的内容，疑似通过 AI 对我移植版本进行了洗稿。其实现质量较低，对性能未做实质优化，存在暴力降低分辨率且不可调、过度使用 `blur` 滤镜、未处理浏览器默认点击行为、对话框布局异常、渲染锯齿明显等问题。\n\n   尤为值得注意的是，GooseCode 将其 GooseHyperGlass 项目链接置于所谓“官网”展示列表的第一项，却在介绍文案中完全隐去原始作者信息（Kyant、Z.AI 与我）。 这种将项目置于最显眼位置却刻意抹除来源的做法，进一步剥夺了用户的知情权，也加深了“此为 GooseCode 独立原创”的误导印象。\n\n5. 宣传页面的署名缺失\n\n   我发现该项目在宣传页面（官网、Kimi 社区动态）中未在显著位置标注原作者 Kyant 及我的移植贡献。项目名称 GooseHyperGlass 具有较强的误导性，容易让人误以为是 GooseCode 的独立原创作品。\n\n6. 我的沟通与要求\n\n   我联系对方，要求其在宣传页面的显著位置重新标注原作者、修改项目名称以避免混淆，并对当前低质量的实现进行优化或下架处理。\n\n7. 对方的有限调整\n\n   对方在页面中做了少量调整（如在 README 角落添加了小字），但始终未修改项目名称，也未在宣传页面的显著位置补充完整署名。\n\n8. GooseCode 在 Kyant 仓库提交的 Issue\n\n   就在上述沟通期间，GooseCode 在 Kyant 的 AndroidLiquidGlass 原始仓库中提交了一个 Issue，指控 liquid-glass-webgl 存在“未授权分发”和“未署名”的问题。考虑到 GooseCode 自身在 GooseHyperGlass 项目中存在未充分署名的争议，我认为这种在原始作者面前以“维权者”姿态指控他人、却对自身项目的署名缺失避而不谈的行为，是极不妥当的。\n\n9. Kimi 社区的不当言论\n\n   此外，GooseCode 在 Kimi 社区发布动态，将 Kyant 对其 Issue 的礼貌性互动（点赞）片面解读为对其行为的认可，并据此继续发表不当言论。这种对原作者态度的截取与放大，我认为是不尊重原作者的。\n\n---\n\n关于技术实现的补充说明\n\nGooseHyperGlass 当前版本存在多项明显的技术缺陷，包括但不限于：\n- 强制降低渲染分辨率且不提供用户调节选项，导致画面模糊；\n- 抗锯齿处理缺失，边缘锯齿严重；\n- 未阻止浏览器默认点击行为（如蓝色高亮、焦点轮廓）；\n- 整体被施加了一层不自然的蓝色滤镜，偏离了真实液态玻璃的物理透光特性；\n- 基础布局存在缺陷（如对话框高度计算错误）。\n\n这些问题并非风格选择，而是实现不完整的客观表现。\n\n---\n\n我的看法\n\n我认为 GooseCode 在这件事上至少存在以下三个方面的问题：\n\n品行方面：将他人投入数十小时的劳动成果冠以自身品牌（GooseHyperGlass），在宣传文案中系统性抹除上游作者；在被指出后仅做表面文章（README 角落小字），拒绝修正核心问题（项目名称与宣传页面署名）；更在原始作者面前扮演“开源警察”角色，对他人的署名问题横加指责，却对自身项目的署名缺失视而不见。\n\n道德方面：开源社区建立在信任、尊重与透明之上。无论采用何种许可证，署名（Attribution）都是不可让渡的底线。通过误导性命名和宣传，让用户产生“独立原创”的错觉，实质上是将社区共享的知识成果私有化，违背了开源精神的基本伦理。\n\n技术方面：即使借助 AI 辅助开发，GooseHyperGlass 的实现质量也反映出对底层渲染原理缺乏基本理解——以暴力降分辨率掩盖性能问题，以无限堆叠 `blur` 和蓝色滤镜冒充“液态玻璃”，连 G2 连续曲率圆角和浏览器默认行为处理都未能正确实现。结合其过往项目的技术深度来看，这种“只会加 blur”的封装方式，难以称之为专业的技术产出。\n\n---\n\n当前状态\n\n目前，我已为自己的移植仓库补充了完整的 `README.md` 和 Apache 2.0 LICENSE 文件，并明确标注了 Kyant 作为原始作者。我希望开源社区能够基于事实，对 GooseHyperGlass 项目的归属和署名问题有一个清晰的认知。\n\n---\n\n期望\n\n我希望 GooseCode 能够：\n\n1. 在官网及 Kimi 社区动态等宣传页面的显著位置，明确标注该技术基于 Kyant 的 AndroidLiquidGlass 及我的移植工作；\n2. 修改 GooseHyperGlass 这一具有误导性的项目名称；\n3. 对当前低质量的实现进行实质性优化，或下架该项目。\n\n感谢大家的阅读。\n\n---\n\n---\n\nClarification regarding the GooseHyperGlass project and the liquid-glass-webgl port\n\nHi everyone, I would like to provide an objective and complete clarification regarding the relationship between the GooseHyperGlass project recently published by GooseCode ([@Minecraftgoose](https://github.com/Minecraftgoose)) and my earlier port, [liquid-glass-webgl](https://github.com/martin65536/liquid-glass-webgl).\n\n---\n\nBackground\n\nAfter discovering Kyant's excellent [AndroidLiquidGlass](https://github.com/Kyant0/AndroidLiquidGlass) project, I decided to port it to the Web. I spent approximately two weeks and dozens of hours on this work. Since I used the Z.AI agent for most of the development process, I focused primarily on visual fidelity and performance optimization, which led me to initially omit the `README.md` and `LICENSE` files. However, on my release pages ([Bilibili BV14ZNn6QED5](https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV14ZNn6QED5) and Kimi community posts), I placed the original author Kyant's credit in the most prominent position. There was never any intent to conceal the original author. (The Kimi community page does not have a direct share link; relevant screenshots are attached below.)\n\n---\n\nTimeline\n\n1. Initial Technical Exchange\n\n   I noticed that the corner radius implementation on GooseCode's official website had noticeable flaws. In good faith, I suggested that GooseCode could refer to the G2 continuous curvature implementation in my ported repository.\n\n2. GooseCode's Response\n\n   Rather than accepting the suggestion, the other party questioned the performance of my port, claiming that \"using WebGL would definitely prevent smooth operation on A9 devices.\"\n\n3. My Stance\n\n   In response, I welcomed them to produce a smoother and higher-quality implementation.\n\n4. Release of GooseHyperGlass and Suspected Plagiarism\n\n   Shortly after, GooseCode released the GooseHyperGlass project. Upon comparison, this project shows highly similar content to my Z.AI-assisted port in terms of shader code structure and core algorithms, strongly suggesting that it was AI-repurposed (\"washed\") from my port. The implementation quality is notably low, with no substantive performance optimization, featuring forcibly reduced resolution without user adjustment, excessive use of `blur` filters, unhandled browser default click behaviors, broken dialog layouts, and prominent rendering aliasing.\n\n   Of particular concern is that GooseCode placed the GooseHyperGlass project link at the top of their so-called \"official website\" showcase list, yet completely omitted the original authors (Kyant, Z.AI, and me) from the descriptive text. This practice of featuring a project most prominently while deliberately erasing its origin further misleads users into believing it is an original work by GooseCode.\n\n5. Lack of Attribution on Promotional Pages\n\n   I noticed that the project's promotional pages (official website and Kimi community posts) did not credit the original author Kyant or my porting contribution in a prominent manner. The name \"GooseHyperGlass\" is also misleading, suggesting an independent original work by GooseCode.\n\n6. My Request for Rectification\n\n   I contacted GooseCode, requesting prominent attribution on promotional pages, a name change to avoid confusion, and either substantive optimization or removal of the project.\n\n7. Limited Adjustments by GooseCode\n\n   While minor adjustments were made (e.g., adding small text in a corner of the README), the project name was not changed, and promotional pages still lack proper, prominent attribution.\n\n8. GooseCode's Issue on Kyant's Repository\n\n   During the above communication, GooseCode submitted an Issue to Kyant's original AndroidLiquidGlass repository, accusing liquid-glass-webgl of \"unauthorized redistribution\" and \"lack of attribution.\" Given the ongoing attribution disputes surrounding GooseHyperGlass itself, I find this behavior—posing as an enforcer of open-source ethics before the original author while ignoring their own project's attribution failures—to be highly inappropriate.\n\n9. Inappropriate Statements on Kimi Community\n\n   Furthermore, GooseCode posted on the Kimi community, interpreting Kyant's polite interaction (a like on the Issue) as endorsement of their actions, and continued to make inappropriate statements. I believe this selective framing of the original author's attitude is disrespectful.\n\n---\n\nAdditional Notes on Technical Implementation\n\nThe current version of GooseHyperGlass suffers from several objective technical deficiencies, including but not limited to:\n- Forcibly reduced rendering resolution without user-adjustable options, resulting in blurry output;\n- Missing anti-aliasing, causing severe edge aliasing;\n- Failure to suppress browser default behaviors (e.g., blue tap highlights, focus outlines);\n- An unnatural blue color filter applied globally, deviating from the physical light transmission properties of real liquid glass;\n- Fundamental layout defects (e.g., incorrect dialog height calculations).\n\nThese are not stylistic choices, but objective indicators of an incomplete implementation.\n\n---\n\nMy Assessment\n\nI believe GooseCode's conduct in this matter is problematic in at least three dimensions:\n\nCharacter: Appropriating the fruits of others' labor under their own brand (GooseHyperGlass), systematically erasing upstream authors from promotional materials, and making only cosmetic changes (tiny README footnotes) when called out—while refusing to address core issues such as the project name and promotional attribution. Furthermore, posing as an \"open-source police officer\" to accuse others of attribution violations before the original author, while remaining silent about their own project's lack of proper credit.\n\nEthics: Open source is built on trust, respect, and transparency. Regardless of the license used, attribution is a non-negotiable baseline. Using misleading naming and promotional copy to create the illusion of independent originality effectively privatizes community-shared knowledge and violates the fundamental ethics of open source.\n\nTechnical competence: Even with AI assistance, the quality of GooseHyperGlass reveals a lack of understanding of underlying rendering principles—masking performance issues by forcibly lowering resolution, imitating \"liquid glass\" with excessive `blur` and blue filters, and failing to implement basic requirements such as G2 continuous curvature corners and browser default behavior handling. Taken together with the technical depth demonstrated in their previous projects, this \"blur-stacking\" approach to packaging can hardly be called professional technical output.\n\n---\n\nCurrent Status\n\nI have now added a complete `README.md` and Apache 2.0 LICENSE to my port repository, clearly crediting Kyant as the original author. I hope the open-source community can form a clear understanding of the provenance and attribution issues surrounding GooseHyperGlass.\n\n---\n\nExpectations\n\nI hope GooseCode will:\n\n1. Prominently credit the technology as based on Kyant's AndroidLiquidGlass and my porting work on all promotional pages (official website and Kimi community);\n2. Change the misleading project name \"GooseHyperGlass\";\n3. Either substantially optimize the current low-quality implementation or remove the project.\n\nThank you for reading.\n\n\u003cimg width=\"1080\" height=\"2400\" alt=\"Image\" src=\"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2a2c13b3-35cc-4816-b61a-b04f9c89f29e\" /\u003e\n\u003cimg width=\"1080\" height=\"2400\" alt=\"Image\" src=\"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/58690dd3-d829-4c89-9a88-216bb36e5c5e\" /\u003e\n\u003cimg width=\"1080\" height=\"2400\" alt=\"Image\" src=\"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ef9fed7f-7850-4f8f-b022-10980b99540d\" /\u003e\n\u003cimg width=\"1080\" height=\"2400\" alt=\"Image\" src=\"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2d243250-8db2-4fe6-a51e-97841b0a8b78\" /\u003e\n\u003cimg width=\"1080\" height=\"2400\" alt=\"Image\" src=\"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/23c4c379-ff02-4699-acff-7210537e2d8b\" /\u003e\n\u003cimg width=\"1080\" height=\"2400\" alt=\"Image\" src=\"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d9134251-9659-4e8a-b3e1-f39fc23a3133\" /\u003e\n\u003cimg width=\"1080\" height=\"2400\" alt=\"Image\" src=\"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26df3f59-8cb8-4219-adfb-beadacabaa19\" /\u003e\n\u003cimg width=\"1080\" height=\"2400\" alt=\"Image\" src=\"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/efc09d92-8913-4fe3-aead-2b69967ca50f\" /\u003e\n\u003cimg width=\"1080\" height=\"2400\" alt=\"Image\" src=\"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ec6a3347-c725-4ae4-b1df-fbe87647f170\" /\u003e","author":{"url":"https://github.com/martin65536","@type":"Person","name":"martin65536"},"datePublished":"2026-07-23T19:33:03.000Z","interactionStatistic":{"@type":"InteractionCounter","interactionType":"https://schema.org/CommentAction","userInteractionCount":0},"url":"https://github.com/112/AndroidLiquidGlass/issues/112"}</script>

  <script type="application/json" data-target="react-app.embeddedData">{"payload":{"preloaded_records":{},"structured_data":{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"DiscussionForumPosting","headline":"关于 GooseHyperGlass 项目与 liquid-glass-webgl 移植仓库的关系说明","articleBody":"大家好，我想就近期 GooseCode（[@Minecraftgoose](https://github.com/Minecraftgoose)）发布的 [GooseHyperGlass](https://github.com/Minecraftgoose/GooseHyperGlass) 项目与我之前移植的 [liquid-glass-webgl](https://github.com/martin65536/liquid-glass-webgl) 之间的关系，做一个客观、完整的说明，以便社区厘清事实。\n\n---\n\n背景\n\n我在发现 Kyant 的 [AndroidLiquidGlass](https://github.com/Kyant0/AndroidLiquidGlass) 项目后，认为其视觉效果与设计理念非常出色，于是投入约两周时间、数十小时将其移植到 Web 端。由于移植过程中几乎全程使用 Z.AI agent 辅助，我将主要精力放在了效果还原与性能优化上，疏忽了仓库的文档建设，初期未添加 `README.md` 和 `LICENSE` 文件。但我在 [Bilibili（BV14ZNn6QED5）](https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV14ZNn6QED5) 和 Kimi 社区等平台的发布页面中，均将原作者 Kyant 的署名置于最显著位置，不存在任何隐藏原作者的意图。（Kimi 社区页面无直接分享链接，相关截图可见下方附件。）\n\n---\n\n事件经过\n\n1. 技术交流的初衷\n\n   我注意到 GooseCode 官网的圆角实现存在明显瑕疵，出于技术交流的目的，我主动向 GooseCode 建议可以参考我移植仓库中的 G2 连续曲率圆角实现。\n\n2. 对方的回应\n\n   对方并未接受建议，反而质疑我移植版本的性能，声称“用了 WebGL 就肯定在 A9 设备上无法流畅运行”。\n\n3. 我的表态\n\n   我在此背景下表示，欢迎对方做出更流畅、更优质的实现。\n\n4. GooseHyperGlass 的发布与疑似洗稿\n\n   随后 GooseCode 发布了 GooseHyperGlass 项目。经对比，该项目在 Shader 代码结构、核心算法等方面存在大量与我基于 Z.AI 移植的版本高度相似的内容，疑似通过 AI 对我移植版本进行了洗稿。其实现质量较低，对性能未做实质优化，存在暴力降低分辨率且不可调、过度使用 `blur` 滤镜、未处理浏览器默认点击行为、对话框布局异常、渲染锯齿明显等问题。\n\n   尤为值得注意的是，GooseCode 将其 GooseHyperGlass 项目链接置于所谓“官网”展示列表的第一项，却在介绍文案中完全隐去原始作者信息（Kyant、Z.AI 与我）。 这种将项目置于最显眼位置却刻意抹除来源的做法，进一步剥夺了用户的知情权，也加深了“此为 GooseCode 独立原创”的误导印象。\n\n5. 宣传页面的署名缺失\n\n   我发现该项目在宣传页面（官网、Kimi 社区动态）中未在显著位置标注原作者 Kyant 及我的移植贡献。项目名称 GooseHyperGlass 具有较强的误导性，容易让人误以为是 GooseCode 的独立原创作品。\n\n6. 我的沟通与要求\n\n   我联系对方，要求其在宣传页面的显著位置重新标注原作者、修改项目名称以避免混淆，并对当前低质量的实现进行优化或下架处理。\n\n7. 对方的有限调整\n\n   对方在页面中做了少量调整（如在 README 角落添加了小字），但始终未修改项目名称，也未在宣传页面的显著位置补充完整署名。\n\n8. GooseCode 在 Kyant 仓库提交的 Issue\n\n   就在上述沟通期间，GooseCode 在 Kyant 的 AndroidLiquidGlass 原始仓库中提交了一个 Issue，指控 liquid-glass-webgl 存在“未授权分发”和“未署名”的问题。考虑到 GooseCode 自身在 GooseHyperGlass 项目中存在未充分署名的争议，我认为这种在原始作者面前以“维权者”姿态指控他人、却对自身项目的署名缺失避而不谈的行为，是极不妥当的。\n\n9. Kimi 社区的不当言论\n\n   此外，GooseCode 在 Kimi 社区发布动态，将 Kyant 对其 Issue 的礼貌性互动（点赞）片面解读为对其行为的认可，并据此继续发表不当言论。这种对原作者态度的截取与放大，我认为是不尊重原作者的。\n\n---\n\n关于技术实现的补充说明\n\nGooseHyperGlass 当前版本存在多项明显的技术缺陷，包括但不限于：\n- 强制降低渲染分辨率且不提供用户调节选项，导致画面模糊；\n- 抗锯齿处理缺失，边缘锯齿严重；\n- 未阻止浏览器默认点击行为（如蓝色高亮、焦点轮廓）；\n- 整体被施加了一层不自然的蓝色滤镜，偏离了真实液态玻璃的物理透光特性；\n- 基础布局存在缺陷（如对话框高度计算错误）。\n\n这些问题并非风格选择，而是实现不完整的客观表现。\n\n---\n\n我的看法\n\n我认为 GooseCode 在这件事上至少存在以下三个方面的问题：\n\n品行方面：将他人投入数十小时的劳动成果冠以自身品牌（GooseHyperGlass），在宣传文案中系统性抹除上游作者；在被指出后仅做表面文章（README 角落小字），拒绝修正核心问题（项目名称与宣传页面署名）；更在原始作者面前扮演“开源警察”角色，对他人的署名问题横加指责，却对自身项目的署名缺失视而不见。\n\n道德方面：开源社区建立在信任、尊重与透明之上。无论采用何种许可证，署名（Attribution）都是不可让渡的底线。通过误导性命名和宣传，让用户产生“独立原创”的错觉，实质上是将社区共享的知识成果私有化，违背了开源精神的基本伦理。\n\n技术方面：即使借助 AI 辅助开发，GooseHyperGlass 的实现质量也反映出对底层渲染原理缺乏基本理解——以暴力降分辨率掩盖性能问题，以无限堆叠 `blur` 和蓝色滤镜冒充“液态玻璃”，连 G2 连续曲率圆角和浏览器默认行为处理都未能正确实现。结合其过往项目的技术深度来看，这种“只会加 blur”的封装方式，难以称之为专业的技术产出。\n\n---\n\n当前状态\n\n目前，我已为自己的移植仓库补充了完整的 `README.md` 和 Apache 2.0 LICENSE 文件，并明确标注了 Kyant 作为原始作者。我希望开源社区能够基于事实，对 GooseHyperGlass 项目的归属和署名问题有一个清晰的认知。\n\n---\n\n期望\n\n我希望 GooseCode 能够：\n\n1. 在官网及 Kimi 社区动态等宣传页面的显著位置，明确标注该技术基于 Kyant 的 AndroidLiquidGlass 及我的移植工作；\n2. 修改 GooseHyperGlass 这一具有误导性的项目名称；\n3. 对当前低质量的实现进行实质性优化，或下架该项目。\n\n感谢大家的阅读。\n\n---\n\n---\n\nClarification regarding the GooseHyperGlass project and the liquid-glass-webgl port\n\nHi everyone, I would like to provide an objective and complete clarification regarding the relationship between the GooseHyperGlass project recently published by GooseCode ([@Minecraftgoose](https://github.com/Minecraftgoose)) and my earlier port, [liquid-glass-webgl](https://github.com/martin65536/liquid-glass-webgl).\n\n---\n\nBackground\n\nAfter discovering Kyant's excellent [AndroidLiquidGlass](https://github.com/Kyant0/AndroidLiquidGlass) project, I decided to port it to the Web. I spent approximately two weeks and dozens of hours on this work. Since I used the Z.AI agent for most of the development process, I focused primarily on visual fidelity and performance optimization, which led me to initially omit the `README.md` and `LICENSE` files. However, on my release pages ([Bilibili BV14ZNn6QED5](https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV14ZNn6QED5) and Kimi community posts), I placed the original author Kyant's credit in the most prominent position. There was never any intent to conceal the original author. (The Kimi community page does not have a direct share link; relevant screenshots are attached below.)\n\n---\n\nTimeline\n\n1. Initial Technical Exchange\n\n   I noticed that the corner radius implementation on GooseCode's official website had noticeable flaws. In good faith, I suggested that GooseCode could refer to the G2 continuous curvature implementation in my ported repository.\n\n2. GooseCode's Response\n\n   Rather than accepting the suggestion, the other party questioned the performance of my port, claiming that \"using WebGL would definitely prevent smooth operation on A9 devices.\"\n\n3. My Stance\n\n   In response, I welcomed them to produce a smoother and higher-quality implementation.\n\n4. Release of GooseHyperGlass and Suspected Plagiarism\n\n   Shortly after, GooseCode released the GooseHyperGlass project. Upon comparison, this project shows highly similar content to my Z.AI-assisted port in terms of shader code structure and core algorithms, strongly suggesting that it was AI-repurposed (\"washed\") from my port. The implementation quality is notably low, with no substantive performance optimization, featuring forcibly reduced resolution without user adjustment, excessive use of `blur` filters, unhandled browser default click behaviors, broken dialog layouts, and prominent rendering aliasing.\n\n   Of particular concern is that GooseCode placed the GooseHyperGlass project link at the top of their so-called \"official website\" showcase list, yet completely omitted the original authors (Kyant, Z.AI, and me) from the descriptive text. This practice of featuring a project most prominently while deliberately erasing its origin further misleads users into believing it is an original work by GooseCode.\n\n5. Lack of Attribution on Promotional Pages\n\n   I noticed that the project's promotional pages (official website and Kimi community posts) did not credit the original author Kyant or my porting contribution in a prominent manner. The name \"GooseHyperGlass\" is also misleading, suggesting an independent original work by GooseCode.\n\n6. My Request for Rectification\n\n   I contacted GooseCode, requesting prominent attribution on promotional pages, a name change to avoid confusion, and either substantive optimization or removal of the project.\n\n7. Limited Adjustments by GooseCode\n\n   While minor adjustments were made (e.g., adding small text in a corner of the README), the project name was not changed, and promotional pages still lack proper, prominent attribution.\n\n8. GooseCode's Issue on Kyant's Repository\n\n   During the above communication, GooseCode submitted an Issue to Kyant's original AndroidLiquidGlass repository, accusing liquid-glass-webgl of \"unauthorized redistribution\" and \"lack of attribution.\" Given the ongoing attribution disputes surrounding GooseHyperGlass itself, I find this behavior—posing as an enforcer of open-source ethics before the original author while ignoring their own project's attribution failures—to be highly inappropriate.\n\n9. Inappropriate Statements on Kimi Community\n\n   Furthermore, GooseCode posted on the Kimi community, interpreting Kyant's polite interaction (a like on the Issue) as endorsement of their actions, and continued to make inappropriate statements. I believe this selective framing of the original author's attitude is disrespectful.\n\n---\n\nAdditional Notes on Technical Implementation\n\nThe current version of GooseHyperGlass suffers from several objective technical deficiencies, including but not limited to:\n- Forcibly reduced rendering resolution without user-adjustable options, resulting in blurry output;\n- Missing anti-aliasing, causing severe edge aliasing;\n- Failure to suppress browser default behaviors (e.g., blue tap highlights, focus outlines);\n- An unnatural blue color filter applied globally, deviating from the physical light transmission properties of real liquid glass;\n- Fundamental layout defects (e.g., incorrect dialog height calculations).\n\nThese are not stylistic choices, but objective indicators of an incomplete implementation.\n\n---\n\nMy Assessment\n\nI believe GooseCode's conduct in this matter is problematic in at least three dimensions:\n\nCharacter: Appropriating the fruits of others' labor under their own brand (GooseHyperGlass), systematically erasing upstream authors from promotional materials, and making only cosmetic changes (tiny README footnotes) when called out—while refusing to address core issues such as the project name and promotional attribution. Furthermore, posing as an \"open-source police officer\" to accuse others of attribution violations before the original author, while remaining silent about their own project's lack of proper credit.\n\nEthics: Open source is built on trust, respect, and transparency. Regardless of the license used, attribution is a non-negotiable baseline. Using misleading naming and promotional copy to create the illusion of independent originality effectively privatizes community-shared knowledge and violates the fundamental ethics of open source.\n\nTechnical competence: Even with AI assistance, the quality of GooseHyperGlass reveals a lack of understanding of underlying rendering principles—masking performance issues by forcibly lowering resolution, imitating \"liquid glass\" with excessive `blur` and blue filters, and failing to implement basic requirements such as G2 continuous curvature corners and browser default behavior handling. Taken together with the technical depth demonstrated in their previous projects, this \"blur-stacking\" approach to packaging can hardly be called professional technical output.\n\n---\n\nCurrent Status\n\nI have now added a complete `README.md` and Apache 2.0 LICENSE to my port repository, clearly crediting Kyant as the original author. I hope the open-source community can form a clear understanding of the provenance and attribution issues surrounding GooseHyperGlass.\n\n---\n\nExpectations\n\nI hope GooseCode will:\n\n1. Prominently credit the technology as based on Kyant's AndroidLiquidGlass and my porting work on all promotional pages (official website and Kimi community);\n2. Change the misleading project name \"GooseHyperGlass\";\n3. Either substantially optimize the current low-quality implementation or remove the project.\n\nThank you for reading.\n\n\u003cimg width=\"1080\" height=\"2400\" alt=\"Image\" GooseCode 官网的圆角实现存在明显瑕疵，出于技术交流的目的，我主动向 GooseCode 建议可以参考我移植仓库中的 G2 连续曲率圆角实现。\n\n2. 对方的回应\n\n   对方并未接受建议，反而质疑我移植版本的性能，声称“用了 WebGL 就肯定在 A9 设备上无法流畅运行”。\n\n3. 我的表态\n\n   我在此背景下表示，欢迎对方做出更流畅、更优质的实现。\n\n4. GooseHyperGlass 的发布与疑似洗稿\n\n   随后 GooseCode 发布了 GooseHyperGlass 项目。经对比，该项目在 Shader 代码结构、核心算法等方面存在大量与我基于 Z.AI 移植的版本高度相似的内容，疑似通过 AI 对我移植版本进行了洗稿。其实现质量较低，对性能未做实质优化，存在暴力降低分辨率且不可调、过度使用 `blur` 滤镜、未处理浏览器默认点击行为、对话框布局异常、渲染锯齿明显等问题。\n\n   尤为值得注意的是，GooseCode 将其 GooseHyperGlass 项目链接置于所谓“官网”展示列表的第一项，却在介绍文案中完全隐去原始作者信息（Kyant、Z.AI 与我）。 这种将项目置于最显眼位置却刻意抹除来源的做法，进一步剥夺了用户的知情权，也加深了“此为 GooseCode 独立原创”的误导印象。\n\n5. 宣传页面的署名缺失\n\n   我发现该项目在宣传页面（官网、Kimi 社区动态）中未在显著位置标注原作者 Kyant 及我的移植贡献。项目名称 GooseHyperGlass 具有较强的误导性，容易让人误以为是 GooseCode 的独立原创作品。\n\n6. 我的沟通与要求\n\n   我联系对方，要求其在宣传页面的显著位置重新标注原作者、修改项目名称以避免混淆，并对当前低质量的实现进行优化或下架处理。\n\n7. 对方的有限调整\n\n   对方在页面中做了少量调整（如在 README 角落添加了小字），但始终未修改项目名称，也未在宣传页面的显著位置补充完整署名。\n\n8. GooseCode 在 Kyant 仓库提交的 Issue\n\n   就在上述沟通期间，GooseCode 在 Kyant 的 AndroidLiquidGlass 原始仓库中提交了一个 Issue，指控 liquid-glass-webgl 存在“未授权分发”和“未署名”的问题。考虑到 GooseCode 自身在 GooseHyperGlass 项目中存在未充分署名的争议，我认为这种在原始作者面前以“维权者”姿态指控他人、却对自身项目的署名缺失避而不谈的行为，是极不妥当的。\n\n9. Kimi 社区的不当言论\n\n   此外，GooseCode 在 Kimi 社区发布动态，将 Kyant 对其 Issue 的礼貌性互动（点赞）片面解读为对其行为的认可，并据此继续发表不当言论。这种对原作者态度的截取与放大，我认为是不尊重原作者的。\n\n---\n\n关于技术实现的补充说明\n\nGooseHyperGlass 当前版本存在多项明显的技术缺陷，包括但不限于：\n- 强制降低渲染分辨率且不提供用户调节选项，导致画面模糊；\n- 抗锯齿处理缺失，边缘锯齿严重；\n- 未阻止浏览器默认点击行为（如蓝色高亮、焦点轮廓）；\n- 整体被施加了一层不自然的蓝色滤镜，偏离了真实液态玻璃的物理透光特性；\n- 基础布局存在缺陷（如对话框高度计算错误）。\n\n这些问题并非风格选择，而是实现不完整的客观表现。\n\n---\n\n我的看法\n\n我认为 GooseCode 在这件事上至少存在以下三个方面的问题：\n\n品行方面：将他人投入数十小时的劳动成果冠以自身品牌（GooseHyperGlass），在宣传文案中系统性抹除上游作者；在被指出后仅做表面文章（README 角落小字），拒绝修正核心问题（项目名称与宣传页面署名）；更在原始作者面前扮演“开源警察”角色，对他人的署名问题横加指责，却对自身项目的署名缺失视而不见。\n\n道德方面：开源社区建立在信任、尊重与透明之上。无论采用何种许可证，署名（Attribution）都是不可让渡的底线。通过误导性命名和宣传，让用户产生“独立原创”的错觉，实质上是将社区共享的知识成果私有化，违背了开源精神的基本伦理。\n\n技术方面：即使借助 AI 辅助开发，GooseHyperGlass 的实现质量也反映出对底层渲染原理缺乏基本理解——以暴力降分辨率掩盖性能问题，以无限堆叠 `blur` 和蓝色滤镜冒充“液态玻璃”，连 G2 连续曲率圆角和浏览器默认行为处理都未能正确实现。结合其过往项目的技术深度来看，这种“只会加 blur”的封装方式，难以称之为专业的技术产出。\n\n---\n\n当前状态\n\n目前，我已为自己的移植仓库补充了完整的 `README.md` 和 Apache 2.0 LICENSE 文件，并明确标注了 Kyant 作为原始作者。我希望开源社区能够基于事实，对 GooseHyperGlass 项目的归属和署名问题有一个清晰的认知。\n\n---\n\n期望\n\n我希望 GooseCode 能够：\n\n1. 在官网及 Kimi 社区动态等宣传页面的显著位置，明确标注该技术基于 Kyant 的 AndroidLiquidGlass 及我的移植工作；\n2. 修改 GooseHyperGlass 这一具有误导性的项目名称；\n3. 对当前低质量的实现进行实质性优化，或下架该项目。\n\n感谢大家的阅读。\n\n---\n\n---\n\nClarification regarding the GooseHyperGlass project and the liquid-glass-webgl port\n\nHi everyone, I would like to provide an objective and complete clarification regarding the relationship between the GooseHyperGlass project recently published by GooseCode ([@Minecraftgoose](https://github.com/Minecraftgoose)) and my earlier port, [liquid-glass-webgl](https://github.com/martin65536/liquid-glass-webgl).\n\n---\n\nBackground\n\nAfter discovering Kyant's excellent [AndroidLiquidGlass](https://github.com/Kyant0/AndroidLiquidGlass) project, I decided to port it to the Web. I spent approximately two weeks and dozens of hours on this work. Since I used the Z.AI agent for most of the development process, I focused primarily on visual fidelity and performance optimization, which led me to initially omit the `README.md` and `LICENSE` files. However, on my release pages ([Bilibili BV14ZNn6QED5](https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV14ZNn6QED5) and Kimi community posts), I placed the original author Kyant's credit in the most prominent position. There was never any intent to conceal the original author. (The Kimi community page does not have a direct share link; relevant screenshots are attached below.)\n\n---\n\nTimeline\n\n1. Initial Technical Exchange\n\n   I noticed that the corner radius implementation on GooseCode's official website had noticeable flaws. In good faith, I suggested that GooseCode could refer to the G2 continuous curvature implementation in my ported repository.\n\n2. GooseCode's Response\n\n   Rather than accepting the suggestion, the other party questioned the performance of my port, claiming that \"using WebGL would definitely prevent smooth operation on A9 devices.\"\n\n3. My Stance\n\n   In response, I welcomed them to produce a smoother and higher-quality implementation.\n\n4. Release of GooseHyperGlass and Suspected Plagiarism\n\n   Shortly after, GooseCode released the GooseHyperGlass project. Upon comparison, this project shows highly similar content to my Z.AI-assisted port in terms of shader code structure and core algorithms, strongly suggesting that it was AI-repurposed (\"washed\") from my port. The implementation quality is notably low, with no substantive performance optimization, featuring forcibly reduced resolution without user adjustment, excessive use of `blur` filters, unhandled browser default click behaviors, broken dialog layouts, and prominent rendering aliasing.\n\n   Of particular concern is that GooseCode placed the GooseHyperGlass project link at the top of their so-called \"official website\" showcase list, yet completely omitted the original authors (Kyant, Z.AI, and me) from the descriptive text. This practice of featuring a project most prominently while deliberately erasing its origin further misleads users into believing it is an original work by GooseCode.\n\n5. Lack of Attribution on Promotional Pages\n\n   I noticed that the project's promotional pages (official website and Kimi community posts) did not credit the original author Kyant or my porting contribution in a prominent manner. The name \"GooseHyperGlass\" is also misleading, suggesting an independent original work by GooseCode.\n\n6. My Request for Rectification\n\n   I contacted GooseCode, requesting prominent attribution on promotional pages, a name change to avoid confusion, and either substantive optimization or removal of the project.\n\n7. Limited Adjustments by GooseCode\n\n   While minor adjustments were made (e.g., adding small text in a corner of the README), the project name was not changed, and promotional pages still lack proper, prominent attribution.\n\n8. GooseCode's Issue on Kyant's Repository\n\n   During the above communication, GooseCode submitted an Issue to Kyant's original AndroidLiquidGlass repository, accusing liquid-glass-webgl of \"unauthorized redistribution\" and \"lack of attribution.\" Given the ongoing attribution disputes surrounding GooseHyperGlass itself, I find this behavior—posing as an enforcer of open-source ethics before the original author while ignoring their own project's attribution failures—to be highly inappropriate.\n\n9. Inappropriate Statements on Kimi Community\n\n   Furthermore, GooseCode posted on the Kimi community, interpreting Kyant's polite interaction (a like on the Issue) as endorsement of their actions, and continued to make inappropriate statements. I believe this selective framing of the original author's attitude is disrespectful.\n\n---\n\nAdditional Notes on Technical Implementation\n\nThe current version of GooseHyperGlass suffers from several objective technical deficiencies, including but not limited to:\n- Forcibly reduced rendering resolution without user-adjustable options, resulting in blurry output;\n- Missing anti-aliasing, causing severe edge aliasing;\n- Failure to suppress browser default behaviors (e.g., blue tap highlights, focus outlines);\n- An unnatural blue color filter applied globally, deviating from the physical light transmission properties of real liquid glass;\n- Fundamental layout defects (e.g., incorrect dialog height calculations).\n\nThese are not stylistic choices, but objective indicators of an incomplete implementation.\n\n---\n\nMy Assessment\n\nI believe GooseCode's conduct in this matter is problematic in at least three dimensions:\n\nCharacter: Appropriating the fruits of others' labor under their own brand (GooseHyperGlass), systematically erasing upstream authors from promotional materials, and making only cosmetic changes (tiny README footnotes) when called out—while refusing to address core issues such as the project name and promotional attribution. Furthermore, posing as an \"open-source police officer\" to accuse others of attribution violations before the original author, while remaining silent about their own project's lack of proper credit.\n\nEthics: Open source is built on trust, respect, and transparency. Regardless of the license used, attribution is a non-negotiable baseline. Using misleading naming and promotional copy to create the illusion of independent originality effectively privatizes community-shared knowledge and violates the fundamental ethics of open source.\n\nTechnical competence: Even with AI assistance, the quality of GooseHyperGlass reveals a lack of understanding of underlying rendering principles—masking performance issues by forcibly lowering resolution, imitating \"liquid glass\" with excessive `blur` and blue filters, and failing to implement basic requirements such as G2 continuous curvature corners and browser default behavior handling. Taken together with the technical depth demonstrated in their previous projects, this \"blur-stacking\" approach to packaging can hardly be called professional technical output.\n\n---\n\nCurrent Status\n\nI have now added a complete `README.md` and Apache 2.0 LICENSE to my port repository, clearly crediting Kyant as the original author. I hope the open-source community can form a clear understanding of the provenance and attribution issues surrounding GooseHyperGlass.\n\n---\n\nExpectations\n\nI hope GooseCode will:\n\n1. Prominently credit the technology as based on Kyant's AndroidLiquidGlass and my porting work on all promotional pages (official website and Kimi community);\n2. Change the misleading project name \"GooseHyperGlass\";\n3. Either substantially optimize the current low-quality implementation or remove the project.\n\nThank you for reading.\n\n\u003cimg width=\"1080\" height=\"2400\" alt=\"Image\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e强制降低渲染分辨率且不提供用户调节选项，导致画面模糊；\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e抗锯齿处理缺失，边缘锯齿严重；\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e未阻止浏览器默认点击行为（如蓝色高亮、焦点轮廓）；\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e整体被施加了一层不自然的蓝色滤镜，偏离了真实液态玻璃的物理透光特性；\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e基础布局存在缺陷（如对话框高度计算错误）。\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e这些问题并非风格选择，而是实现不完整的客观表现。\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e我的看法\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e我认为 GooseCode 在这件事上至少存在以下三个方面的问题：\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e品行方面：将他人投入数十小时的劳动成果冠以自身品牌（GooseHyperGlass），在宣传文案中系统性抹除上游作者；在被指出后仅做表面文章（README 角落小字），拒绝修正核心问题（项目名称与宣传页面署名）；更在原始作者面前扮演“开源警察”角色，对他人的署名问题横加指责，却对自身项目的署名缺失视而不见。\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e道德方面：开源社区建立在信任、尊重与透明之上。无论采用何种许可证，署名（Attribution）都是不可让渡的底线。通过误导性命名和宣传，让用户产生“独立原创”的错觉，实质上是将社区共享的知识成果私有化，违背了开源精神的基本伦理。\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e技术方面：即使借助 AI 辅助开发，GooseHyperGlass 的实现质量也反映出对底层渲染原理缺乏基本理解——以暴力降分辨率掩盖性能问题，以无限堆叠 \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003eblur\u003c/code\u003e 和蓝色滤镜冒充“液态玻璃”，连 G2 连续曲率圆角和浏览器默认行为处理都未能正确实现。结合其过往项目的技术深度来看，这种“只会加 blur”的封装方式，难以称之为专业的技术产出。\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e当前状态\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e目前，我已为自己的移植仓库补充了完整的 \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003eREADME.md\u003c/code\u003e 和 Apache 2.0 LICENSE 文件，并明确标注了 Kyant 作为原始作者。我希望开源社区能够基于事实，对 GooseHyperGlass 项目的归属和署名问题有一个清晰的认知。\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e期望\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e我希望 GooseCode 能够：\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e在官网及 Kimi 社区动态等宣传页面的显著位置，明确标注该技术基于 Kyant 的 AndroidLiquidGlass 及我的移植工作；\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e修改 GooseHyperGlass 这一具有误导性的项目名称；\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e对当前低质量的实现进行实质性优化，或下架该项目。\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e感谢大家的阅读。\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eClarification regarding the GooseHyperGlass project and the liquid-glass-webgl port\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eHi everyone, I would like to provide an objective and complete clarification regarding the relationship between the GooseHyperGlass project recently published by GooseCode (\u003ca href=\"https://github.com/Minecraftgoose\"\u003e@Minecraftgoose\u003c/a\u003e) and my earlier port, \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/martin65536/liquid-glass-webgl\"\u003eliquid-glass-webgl\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eBackground\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAfter discovering Kyant's excellent \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/Kyant0/AndroidLiquidGlass\"\u003eAndroidLiquidGlass\u003c/a\u003e project, I decided to port it to the Web. I spent approximately two weeks and dozens of hours on this work. Since I used the Z.AI agent for most of the development process, I focused primarily on visual fidelity and performance optimization, which led me to initially omit the \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003eREADME.md\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003eLICENSE\u003c/code\u003e files. However, on my release pages (\u003ca href=\"https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV14ZNn6QED5\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eBilibili BV14ZNn6QED5\u003c/a\u003e and Kimi community posts), I placed the original author Kyant's credit in the most prominent position. There was never any intent to conceal the original author. (The Kimi community page does not have a direct share link; relevant screenshots are attached below.)\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTimeline\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eInitial Technical Exchange\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eI noticed that the corner radius implementation on GooseCode's official website had noticeable flaws. In good faith, I suggested that GooseCode could refer to the G2 continuous curvature implementation in my ported repository.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eGooseCode's Response\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eRather than accepting the suggestion, the other party questioned the performance of my port, claiming that \"using WebGL would definitely prevent smooth operation on A9 devices.\"\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eMy Stance\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIn response, I welcomed them to produce a smoother and higher-quality implementation.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eRelease of GooseHyperGlass and Suspected Plagiarism\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eShortly after, GooseCode released the GooseHyperGlass project. Upon comparison, this project shows highly similar content to my Z.AI-assisted port in terms of shader code structure and core algorithms, strongly suggesting that it was AI-repurposed (\"washed\") from my port. The implementation quality is notably low, with no substantive performance optimization, featuring forcibly reduced resolution without user adjustment, excessive use of \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003eblur\u003c/code\u003e filters, unhandled browser default click behaviors, broken dialog layouts, and prominent rendering aliasing.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOf particular concern is that GooseCode placed the GooseHyperGlass project link at the top of their so-called \"official website\" showcase list, yet completely omitted the original authors (Kyant, Z.AI, and me) from the descriptive text. This practice of featuring a project most prominently while deliberately erasing its origin further misleads users into believing it is an original work by GooseCode.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eLack of Attribution on Promotional Pages\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eI noticed that the project's promotional pages (official website and Kimi community posts) did not credit the original author Kyant or my porting contribution in a prominent manner. The name \"GooseHyperGlass\" is also misleading, suggesting an independent original work by GooseCode.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eMy Request for Rectification\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eI contacted GooseCode, requesting prominent attribution on promotional pages, a name change to avoid confusion, and either substantive optimization or removal of the project.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eLimited Adjustments by GooseCode\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhile minor adjustments were made (e.g., adding small text in a corner of the README), the project name was not changed, and promotional pages still lack proper, prominent attribution.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eGooseCode's Issue on Kyant's Repository\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eDuring the above communication, GooseCode submitted an Issue to Kyant's original AndroidLiquidGlass repository, accusing liquid-glass-webgl of \"unauthorized redistribution\" and \"lack of attribution.\" Given the ongoing attribution disputes surrounding GooseHyperGlass itself, I find this behavior—posing as an enforcer of open-source ethics before the original author while ignoring their own project's attribution failures—to be highly inappropriate.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eInappropriate Statements on Kimi Community\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFurthermore, GooseCode posted on the Kimi community, interpreting Kyant's polite interaction (a like on the Issue) as endorsement of their actions, and continued to make inappropriate statements. I believe this selective framing of the original author's attitude is disrespectful.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAdditional Notes on Technical Implementation\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe current version of GooseHyperGlass suffers from several objective technical deficiencies, including but not limited to:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eForcibly reduced rendering resolution without user-adjustable options, resulting in blurry output;\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eMissing anti-aliasing, causing severe edge aliasing;\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eFailure to suppress browser default behaviors (e.g., blue tap highlights, focus outlines);\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAn unnatural blue color filter applied globally, deviating from the physical light transmission properties of real liquid glass;\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eFundamental layout defects (e.g., incorrect dialog height calculations).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThese are not stylistic choices, but objective indicators of an incomplete implementation.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eMy Assessment\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eI believe GooseCode's conduct in this matter is problematic in at least three dimensions:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eCharacter: Appropriating the fruits of others' labor under their own brand (GooseHyperGlass), systematically erasing upstream authors from promotional materials, and making only cosmetic changes (tiny README footnotes) when called out—while refusing to address core issues such as the project name and promotional attribution. Furthermore, posing as an \"open-source police officer\" to accuse others of attribution violations before the original author, while remaining silent about their own project's lack of proper credit.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eEthics: Open source is built on trust, respect, and transparency. Regardless of the license used, attribution is a non-negotiable baseline. Using misleading naming and promotional copy to create the illusion of independent originality effectively privatizes community-shared knowledge and violates the fundamental ethics of open source.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTechnical competence: Even with AI assistance, the quality of GooseHyperGlass reveals a lack of understanding of underlying rendering principles—masking performance issues by forcibly lowering resolution, imitating \"liquid glass\" with excessive \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003eblur\u003c/code\u003e and blue filters, and failing to implement basic requirements such as G2 continuous curvature corners and browser default behavior handling. Taken together with the technical depth demonstrated in their previous projects, this \"blur-stacking\" approach to packaging can hardly be called professional technical output.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eCurrent Status\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eI have now added a complete \u003ccode class=\"notranslate\"\u003eREADME.md\u003c/code\u003e and Apache 2.0 LICENSE to my port repository, clearly crediting Kyant as the original author. I hope the open-source community can form a clear understanding of the provenance and attribution issues surrounding GooseHyperGlass.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eExpectations\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eI hope GooseCode will:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eProminently credit the technology as based on Kyant's AndroidLiquidGlass and my porting work on all promotional pages (official website and Kimi community);\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eChange the misleading project name \"GooseHyperGlass\";\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eEither substantially optimize the current low-quality implementation or remove the project.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThank you for reading.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003ca target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" data-team-hovercards-enabled="true" class="markdown-body" data-turbolinks="false"><div class="markdown-body NewMarkdownViewer-module__safe-html-box__ZT1eD"><p dir="auto">大家好，我想就近期 GooseCode（<a href="https://github.com/Minecraftgoose">@Minecraftgoose</a>）发布的 <a href="https://github.com/Minecraftgoose/GooseHyperGlass">GooseHyperGlass</a> 项目与我之前移植的 <a href="https://github.com/martin65536/liquid-glass-webgl">liquid-glass-webgl</a> 之间的关系，做一个客观、完整的说明，以便社区厘清事实。</p>
<hr>
<p dir="auto">背景</p>
<p dir="auto">我在发现 Kyant 的 <a href="https://github.com/Kyant0/AndroidLiquidGlass">AndroidLiquidGlass</a> 项目后，认为其视觉效果与设计理念非常出色，于是投入约两周时间、数十小时将其移植到 Web 端。由于移植过程中几乎全程使用 Z.AI agent 辅助，我将主要精力放在了效果还原与性能优化上，疏忽了仓库的文档建设，初期未添加 <code class="notranslate">README.md</code> 和 <code class="notranslate">LICENSE</code> 文件。但我在 <a href="https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV14ZNn6QED5" rel="nofollow">Bilibili（BV14ZNn6QED5）</a> 和 Kimi 社区等平台的发布页面中，均将原作者 Kyant 的署名置于最显著位置，不存在任何隐藏原作者的意图。（Kimi 社区页面无直接分享链接，相关截图可见下方附件。）</p>
<hr>
<p dir="auto">事件经过</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">技术交流的初衷</p>
<p dir="auto">我注意到 GooseCode 官网的圆角实现存在明显瑕疵，出于技术交流的目的，我主动向 GooseCode 建议可以参考我移植仓库中的 G2 连续曲率圆角实现。</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">对方的回应</p>
<p dir="auto">对方并未接受建议，反而质疑我移植版本的性能，声称“用了 WebGL 就肯定在 A9 设备上无法流畅运行”。</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">我的表态</p>
<p dir="auto">我在此背景下表示，欢迎对方做出更流畅、更优质的实现。</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">GooseHyperGlass 的发布与疑似洗稿</p>
<p dir="auto">随后 GooseCode 发布了 GooseHyperGlass 项目。经对比，该项目在 Shader 代码结构、核心算法等方面存在大量与我基于 Z.AI 移植的版本高度相似的内容，疑似通过 AI 对我移植版本进行了洗稿。其实现质量较低，对性能未做实质优化，存在暴力降低分辨率且不可调、过度使用 <code class="notranslate">blur</code> 滤镜、未处理浏览器默认点击行为、对话框布局异常、渲染锯齿明显等问题。</p>
<p dir="auto">尤为值得注意的是，GooseCode 将其 GooseHyperGlass 项目链接置于所谓“官网”展示列表的第一项，却在介绍文案中完全隐去原始作者信息（Kyant、Z.AI 与我）。 这种将项目置于最显眼位置却刻意抹除来源的做法，进一步剥夺了用户的知情权，也加深了“此为 GooseCode 独立原创”的误导印象。</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">宣传页面的署名缺失</p>
<p dir="auto">我发现该项目在宣传页面（官网、Kimi 社区动态）中未在显著位置标注原作者 Kyant 及我的移植贡献。项目名称 GooseHyperGlass 具有较强的误导性，容易让人误以为是 GooseCode 的独立原创作品。</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">我的沟通与要求</p>
<p dir="auto">我联系对方，要求其在宣传页面的显著位置重新标注原作者、修改项目名称以避免混淆，并对当前低质量的实现进行优化或下架处理。</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">对方的有限调整</p>
<p dir="auto">对方在页面中做了少量调整（如在 README 角落添加了小字），但始终未修改项目名称，也未在宣传页面的显著位置补充完整署名。</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">GooseCode 在 Kyant 仓库提交的 Issue</p>
<p dir="auto">就在上述沟通期间，GooseCode 在 Kyant 的 AndroidLiquidGlass 原始仓库中提交了一个 Issue，指控 liquid-glass-webgl 存在“未授权分发”和“未署名”的问题。考虑到 GooseCode 自身在 GooseHyperGlass 项目中存在未充分署名的争议，我认为这种在原始作者面前以“维权者”姿态指控他人、却对自身项目的署名缺失避而不谈的行为，是极不妥当的。</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">Kimi 社区的不当言论</p>
<p dir="auto">此外，GooseCode 在 Kimi 社区发布动态，将 Kyant 对其 Issue 的礼貌性互动（点赞）片面解读为对其行为的认可，并据此继续发表不当言论。这种对原作者态度的截取与放大，我认为是不尊重原作者的。</p>
</li>
</ol>
<hr>
<p dir="auto">关于技术实现的补充说明</p>
<p dir="auto">GooseHyperGlass 当前版本存在多项明显的技术缺陷，包括但不限于：</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>强制降低渲染分辨率且不提供用户调节选项，导致画面模糊；</li>
<li>抗锯齿处理缺失，边缘锯齿严重；</li>
<li>未阻止浏览器默认点击行为（如蓝色高亮、焦点轮廓）；</li>
<li>整体被施加了一层不自然的蓝色滤镜，偏离了真实液态玻璃的物理透光特性；</li>
<li>基础布局存在缺陷（如对话框高度计算错误）。</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">这些问题并非风格选择，而是实现不完整的客观表现。</p>
<hr>
<p dir="auto">我的看法</p>
<p dir="auto">我认为 GooseCode 在这件事上至少存在以下三个方面的问题：</p>
<p dir="auto">品行方面：将他人投入数十小时的劳动成果冠以自身品牌（GooseHyperGlass），在宣传文案中系统性抹除上游作者；在被指出后仅做表面文章（README 角落小字），拒绝修正核心问题（项目名称与宣传页面署名）；更在原始作者面前扮演“开源警察”角色，对他人的署名问题横加指责，却对自身项目的署名缺失视而不见。</p>
<p dir="auto">道德方面：开源社区建立在信任、尊重与透明之上。无论采用何种许可证，署名（Attribution）都是不可让渡的底线。通过误导性命名和宣传，让用户产生“独立原创”的错觉，实质上是将社区共享的知识成果私有化，违背了开源精神的基本伦理。</p>
<p dir="auto">技术方面：即使借助 AI 辅助开发，GooseHyperGlass 的实现质量也反映出对底层渲染原理缺乏基本理解——以暴力降分辨率掩盖性能问题，以无限堆叠 <code class="notranslate">blur</code> 和蓝色滤镜冒充“液态玻璃”，连 G2 连续曲率圆角和浏览器默认行为处理都未能正确实现。结合其过往项目的技术深度来看，这种“只会加 blur”的封装方式，难以称之为专业的技术产出。</p>
<hr>
<p dir="auto">当前状态</p>
<p dir="auto">目前，我已为自己的移植仓库补充了完整的 <code class="notranslate">README.md</code> 和 Apache 2.0 LICENSE 文件，并明确标注了 Kyant 作为原始作者。我希望开源社区能够基于事实，对 GooseHyperGlass 项目的归属和署名问题有一个清晰的认知。</p>
<hr>
<p dir="auto">期望</p>
<p dir="auto">我希望 GooseCode 能够：</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>在官网及 Kimi 社区动态等宣传页面的显著位置，明确标注该技术基于 Kyant 的 AndroidLiquidGlass 及我的移植工作；</li>
<li>修改 GooseHyperGlass 这一具有误导性的项目名称；</li>
<li>对当前低质量的实现进行实质性优化，或下架该项目。</li>
</ol>
<p dir="auto">感谢大家的阅读。</p>
<hr>
<hr>
<p dir="auto">Clarification regarding the GooseHyperGlass project and the liquid-glass-webgl port</p>
<p dir="auto">Hi everyone, I would like to provide an objective and complete clarification regarding the relationship between the GooseHyperGlass project recently published by GooseCode (<a href="https://github.com/Minecraftgoose">@Minecraftgoose</a>) and my earlier port, <a href="https://github.com/martin65536/liquid-glass-webgl">liquid-glass-webgl</a>.</p>
<hr>
<p dir="auto">Background</p>
<p dir="auto">After discovering Kyant's excellent <a href="https://github.com/Kyant0/AndroidLiquidGlass">AndroidLiquidGlass</a> project, I decided to port it to the Web. I spent approximately two weeks and dozens of hours on this work. Since I used the Z.AI agent for most of the development process, I focused primarily on visual fidelity and performance optimization, which led me to initially omit the <code class="notranslate">README.md</code> and <code class="notranslate">LICENSE</code> files. However, on my release pages (<a href="https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV14ZNn6QED5" rel="nofollow">Bilibili BV14ZNn6QED5</a> and Kimi community posts), I placed the original author Kyant's credit in the most prominent position. There was never any intent to conceal the original author. (The Kimi community page does not have a direct share link; relevant screenshots are attached below.)</p>
<hr>
<p dir="auto">Timeline</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto">Initial Technical Exchange</p>
<p dir="auto">I noticed that the corner radius implementation on GooseCode's official website had noticeable flaws. In good faith, I suggested that GooseCode could refer to the G2 continuous curvature implementation in my ported repository.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">GooseCode's Response</p>
<p dir="auto">Rather than accepting the suggestion, the other party questioned the performance of my port, claiming that "using WebGL would definitely prevent smooth operation on A9 devices."</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">My Stance</p>
<p dir="auto">In response, I welcomed them to produce a smoother and higher-quality implementation.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">Release of GooseHyperGlass and Suspected Plagiarism</p>
<p dir="auto">Shortly after, GooseCode released the GooseHyperGlass project. Upon comparison, this project shows highly similar content to my Z.AI-assisted port in terms of shader code structure and core algorithms, strongly suggesting that it was AI-repurposed ("washed") from my port. The implementation quality is notably low, with no substantive performance optimization, featuring forcibly reduced resolution without user adjustment, excessive use of <code class="notranslate">blur</code> filters, unhandled browser default click behaviors, broken dialog layouts, and prominent rendering aliasing.</p>
<p dir="auto">Of particular concern is that GooseCode placed the GooseHyperGlass project link at the top of their so-called "official website" showcase list, yet completely omitted the original authors (Kyant, Z.AI, and me) from the descriptive text. This practice of featuring a project most prominently while deliberately erasing its origin further misleads users into believing it is an original work by GooseCode.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">Lack of Attribution on Promotional Pages</p>
<p dir="auto">I noticed that the project's promotional pages (official website and Kimi community posts) did not credit the original author Kyant or my porting contribution in a prominent manner. The name "GooseHyperGlass" is also misleading, suggesting an independent original work by GooseCode.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">My Request for Rectification</p>
<p dir="auto">I contacted GooseCode, requesting prominent attribution on promotional pages, a name change to avoid confusion, and either substantive optimization or removal of the project.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">Limited Adjustments by GooseCode</p>
<p dir="auto">While minor adjustments were made (e.g., adding small text in a corner of the README), the project name was not changed, and promotional pages still lack proper, prominent attribution.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">GooseCode's Issue on Kyant's Repository</p>
<p dir="auto">During the above communication, GooseCode submitted an Issue to Kyant's original AndroidLiquidGlass repository, accusing liquid-glass-webgl of "unauthorized redistribution" and "lack of attribution." Given the ongoing attribution disputes surrounding GooseHyperGlass itself, I find this behavior—posing as an enforcer of open-source ethics before the original author while ignoring their own project's attribution failures—to be highly inappropriate.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto">Inappropriate Statements on Kimi Community</p>
<p dir="auto">Furthermore, GooseCode posted on the Kimi community, interpreting Kyant's polite interaction (a like on the Issue) as endorsement of their actions, and continued to make inappropriate statements. I believe this selective framing of the original author's attitude is disrespectful.</p>
</li>
</ol>
<hr>
<p dir="auto">Additional Notes on Technical Implementation</p>
<p dir="auto">The current version of GooseHyperGlass suffers from several objective technical deficiencies, including but not limited to:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Forcibly reduced rendering resolution without user-adjustable options, resulting in blurry output;</li>
<li>Missing anti-aliasing, causing severe edge aliasing;</li>
<li>Failure to suppress browser default behaviors (e.g., blue tap highlights, focus outlines);</li>
<li>An unnatural blue color filter applied globally, deviating from the physical light transmission properties of real liquid glass;</li>
<li>Fundamental layout defects (e.g., incorrect dialog height calculations).</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">These are not stylistic choices, but objective indicators of an incomplete implementation.</p>
<hr>
<p dir="auto">My Assessment</p>
<p dir="auto">I believe GooseCode's conduct in this matter is problematic in at least three dimensions:</p>
<p dir="auto">Character: Appropriating the fruits of others' labor under their own brand (GooseHyperGlass), systematically erasing upstream authors from promotional materials, and making only cosmetic changes (tiny README footnotes) when called out—while refusing to address core issues such as the project name and promotional attribution. Furthermore, posing as an "open-source police officer" to accuse others of attribution violations before the original author, while remaining silent about their own project's lack of proper credit.</p>
<p dir="auto">Ethics: Open source is built on trust, respect, and transparency. Regardless of the license used, attribution is a non-negotiable baseline. Using misleading naming and promotional copy to create the illusion of independent originality effectively privatizes community-shared knowledge and violates the fundamental ethics of open source.</p>
<p dir="auto">Technical competence: Even with AI assistance, the quality of GooseHyperGlass reveals a lack of understanding of underlying rendering principles—masking performance issues by forcibly lowering resolution, imitating "liquid glass" with excessive <code class="notranslate">blur</code> and blue filters, and failing to implement basic requirements such as G2 continuous curvature corners and browser default behavior handling. Taken together with the technical depth demonstrated in their previous projects, this "blur-stacking" approach to packaging can hardly be called professional technical output.</p>
<hr>
<p dir="auto">Current Status</p>
<p dir="auto">I have now added a complete <code class="notranslate">README.md</code> and Apache 2.0 LICENSE to my port repository, clearly crediting Kyant as the original author. I hope the open-source community can form a clear understanding of the provenance and attribution issues surrounding GooseHyperGlass.</p>
<hr>
<p dir="auto">Expectations</p>
<p dir="auto">I hope GooseCode will:</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>Prominently credit the technology as based on Kyant's AndroidLiquidGlass and my porting work on all promotional pages (official website and Kimi community);</li>
<li>Change the misleading project name "GooseHyperGlass";</li>
<li>Either substantially optimize the current low-quality implementation or remove the project.</li>
</ol>

metadata.description: 大家好，我想就近期 GooseCode（@Minecraftgoose）发布的 GooseHyperGlass 项目与我之前移植的 liquid-glass-webgl 之间的关系，做一个客观、完整的说明，以便社区厘清事实。 背景 我在发现 Kyant 的 AndroidLiquidGlass 项目后，认为其视觉效果与设计理念非常出色，于是投入约两周时间、数十小时将其移植到 Web 端。由于移植过程中几乎全程使用 Z.AI agent 辅助，我将主要精力放在了效果还原与...
